about.title: About
about.subtitle: App identity, version, system info, website, GitHub and License.

general.title: General
general.subtitle: Color & display, user profile, assistant and extension entries.

proxy.title: Proxy & port
proxy.subtitle: Set an HTTP proxy for all outbound traffic (AI APIs, search, MCP) and the local service port. Leave the proxy blank to follow the system proxy; leave the port blank to auto-select.
proxy.http_desc: All AI API requests are routed through this proxy
proxy.none_detected_desc: The system proxy is off, or the proxy tool hasn't started yet.
proxy.port_desc: Port the local service listens on, default 8080. Leave blank to auto-select; a fixed port is used when set, walking up if occupied. Takes effect after restart.
proxy.port_invalid: Port must be an integer between 1 and 65535
proxy.port_restart_note: Port changes take effect after restarting. The running instance still uses the old port.

stats.title: Statistics
stats.subtitle: Usage stats and an activity heatmap based on local chats, request logs and model usage.
stats.heatmap_empty: No chat data yet. The heatmap appears once you start chatting.

logs.title: Request logs
logs.subtitle: Endpoint, status code, duration and error summary for every provider request.

search.title: Search services
search.subtitle: Bing and Rikkahub are included by default, with Tavily, Exa, Zhipu and more as templates.
search.name: Name
search.testing_start: Saving config and running the search service test...

speech.tts_subtitle: TTS providers narrate messages; ASR providers power real-time speech recognition in the input box.
speech.asr_subtitle: Supports OpenAI Realtime, DashScope and Volcengine via real-time PCM WebSocket transcription.
speech.asr_empty: No ASR service yet. Click Add to configure one.
speech.asr_card_desc: Configure auth and audio parameters for each ASR provider.
speech.name: Name
speech.silence_duration_desc: Duration used to decide the end of speech.
speech.select_asr: Select or add an ASR service.
speech.tts_empty: No TTS service yet. Click Add to configure one.
speech.tts_card_desc: Message narration uses the current provider and is logged as a request.
speech.test_title: Read a test sentence using the current config
speech.test_text: This is a test voice to verify the current TTS configuration.
speech.test_system_done: Test text played via system voice
speech.system_pitch_desc: System voice pitch can't be adjusted directly; this setting is exported with the config only.
speech.select_tts: Select or add a TTS service.

data.extracting: Upload done, extracting and importing data...
data.pack_slow: With many attachments, packing may take tens of seconds. Please wait.
data.extract_slow: Parsing chat history from a large backup may take a few minutes. Don't close the app.
data.no_schema_warn: Note: the phone-side database schema isn't registered yet, so backups won't include conversations. Export a backup under “Data backup” to register the schema.
data.restore_confirm: Restoring {{name}} overwrites your current local settings, conversations and logs. Continue?
data.s3_restore_confirm: Restore from the remote backup {{name}}? This overwrites the current local state.
data.register_note: Note: this needs to be done once on first use. Re-register when the phone app updates.
data.export_done:
Backup exported: {{name}}
Find it in your browser's default download folder.
data.import_confirm: Importing overwrites the current local settings, conversations and logs. Continue?
data.import_network_error: Network error — is the backend service running?
data.export_with_schema: This export includes {{count}} conversations and all settings, ready to import on the phone.
data.export_without_schema: The phone-side schema isn't registered, so the backup won't include conversations. Register it under “Android compatibility” above first.
data.title: Data
data.subtitle: Local import/export, chat file storage, and WebDAV / S3 remote sync.
data.compat_summary: Phone-side database schema registered (version {{version}}, hash {{hash}}...). PC exports are recognized by the phone.
data.unregistered_warn: The phone-side database schema isn't registered. PC conversations can't be imported to the phone, and S3 / WebDAV backups won't include conversations.
data.current_format: Current format: version {{version}}, hash {{hash}}.... To update, re-upload a phone backup.
data.step1: On the phone, open Rikkahub → Settings → Backup and export a .zip backup
data.step2: Click the button below and pick that .zip to upload
data.step3: The PC extracts only the schema (no data is imported to PC)
data.backup_desc: Export a .zip (Android-compatible) containing settings, conversations, files, Skills, MCP / prompt injections / lorebooks / quick messages. Import also accepts Android .zip and legacy PC .json. Large backups may take 1-2 minutes.
data.chat_files_desc: Uploaded avatars and attachments are saved to the local data directory and served via /api/files.
data.webdav_desc: Test the connection, back up now, list remote backups, restore or delete. Backups include the local JSON state, Skills and uploaded files.
data.no_remote_backups: No remote backups. Click “Refresh” to read the WebDAV directory.
data.no_remote_backups_s3: No remote backups. Click “Refresh” to read the S3 bucket.
data.s3_desc: Standard AWS S3 and compatible endpoints (MinIO/R2/Tencent COS/Alibaba OSS, etc.). Backup contents match WebDAV.
